title: helianthemum variabile subvar. viscosopuberulum
intro: Helianthemum variabile subvar. viscosopuberulum, also known as Sticky Rockrose, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Cistaceae. It is native to the southwestern United States and has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral effects. The plant has been used to treat various health issues such as wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory problems, with potential benefits including reduced inflammation, improved wound healing, and relief from cold and flu symptoms. In herbal preparations, the plant can be made into teas, infusions, and salves, and has also been used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments, although its efficacy and safety have not been extensively scientifically studied.
